questions.stem: Signal flow graph of a system is shown in figure. Determine the value of A, K2, K3 so that the poles of the system lie at: -100 and -0.708 ± j0.7064. Reduce the system to second order using dominant pole pair concept and then determine the value of rise time.
common_errors: Incorrect application of Mason's gain formula to find closed-loop poles
Forgetting to verify dominance condition (far pole ≥ 5× real part of dominant poles)
Error in rise time formula for underdamped 2nd order system: tr = (π - θ)/(ωd)
questions.solution_method: Apply Mason's gain formula to express the closed-loop TF, then equate its denominator to (s+100)(s^2+1.416s+1): set up coefficient equations to solve for A, K2, K3. Drop the far pole at -100 (dominant pair justification), normalize remaining 2nd-order system to standard form, extract ωn and ζ, then compute tr = (π - arccos(ζ))/ωd.

questions.stem: An electro-hydraulic system with lever feedback is shown in figure. Valve orifice coefficient K1, piston area A, flow Q = K1·x − K2·P, LVDT sensitivity KL V/m, input device gain Ki V/m, amplifier gain Ka V/V, transducer gain Kt m/V. (i) Draw the block diagram. (ii) Obtain the transfer function Y(s)/E(s), where y is the displacement of mass M.
common_errors: Missing lever-ratio feedback in block diagram
Incorrect Laplace-domain force-balance on mass M (forgetting B1, B2 damping terms)
Confusing piston flow equation signs leading to wrong loop gain sign
questions.solution_method: Write flow continuity Q = A·sY(s), force balance on mass M: (Ms²+B1s+K1)Y = A·P(s). Express valve opening x from lever geometry using LVDT feedback. Cascade gain blocks (Ki, Ka, KL, Kt) to build the forward-path and feedback-path, then apply Mason or block reduction to get Y(s)/E(s).

questions.stem: The open loop TF of a unity negative feedback system is G(s) = K(s+2)/[s(s²+2s+16)]. Draw the Bode magnitude (asymptotic) and phase plots so that steady-state error of the system is 10%. From the plots, determine gain margin & phase margin and comment on system stability.
common_errors: Incorrect K from SSE: for type-1, ess = 1/Kv = 0.1 → Kv = 10; Kv = lim s·G(s) = K·2/16
Wrong corner frequencies: ωn = 4 (from s²+2s+16), zero at ω=2
Reading GM/PM from asymptotic plot instead of actual phase curve
questions.solution_method: Set ess = 1/Kv = 0.1 → Kv = 10; compute K = 10·(16/2) = 80. Identify corner frequencies: integrator, complex poles at ωn=4 (ζ=0.25), zero at ω=2. Draw asymptotic Bode magnitude; compute phase plot including complex-pole phase contribution. Find phase-crossover frequency (phase=−180°) for GM; find gain-crossover frequency (|G|=0dB) for PM.

questions.stem: The open loop TF of a unity negative feedback system is G(s) = 15/[s(s+4)]. Design a lead compensator so that the system has a phase margin of 70°. Take safety margin as 5°. (Without using Semi-log graph sheet.)
common_errors: Forgetting to add 5° safety margin → φm_required = 70° + 5° = 75°
Using uncompensated PM as current PM without computing it first
Incorrect formula for lead compensator: αmax = (1−sinφmax)/(1+sinφmax)
questions.solution_method: Compute uncompensated PM analytically: find ωgc where |G(jω)|=1, evaluate phase. Required additional phase = 75° − current PM. Find α = (1−sinφmax)/(1+sinφmax). Set new gain-crossover at ωm = 1/(T√α) where |G(jωm)|·(1/√α) = 1. Solve for T. Write compensator Gc(s) = (1+Ts)/(1+αTs).

questions.stem: The block diagram of a control system has forward path K/[s(s+1)] and feedback (Kh·s+1). Sketch neat root contours showing all necessary steps for K > 1 (take any two values) and therefrom determine the time at peak overshoot correspondingly.
common_errors: Confusing root locus (varying K) with root contour (varying Kh for fixed K)
Forgetting to convert fixed-K closed-loop CE into standard KH·(…) = 0 form
Incorrect tp formula: tp = π/ωd; must read ωd from contour
questions.solution_method: For each fixed K > 1, write the closed-loop CE = s²+(1+K·Kh)s+K = 0; rearrange as 1 + Kh·[Ks/(s²+s+K)] = 0 to treat Kh as the root-locus parameter. Sketch contours. From complex pole positions read ζ, ωd. Compute tp = π/ωd for each K value.

questions.stem: A temperature control system has amplifier gain Ka, thermal resistance R (°C/W), thermal capacitance C (J/°C), sensor constant Kt (V/°C), Joule's coefficient J, reference voltage Er. (i) Draw the labeled signal flow graph. (ii) Obtain the incremental transfer function ΔT(s)/ΔEr(s). (iii) Derive sensitivity of the system to Ka changes in open loop and closed loop; reduce for DC conditions.
common_errors: Linearization error: incremental model must perturb Q = J·i² → ΔQ = 2J·I0·Δi
Omitting thermal TF block 1/(RCs+1) in SFG
Sensitivity formula: S^T_Ka = (∂T/∂Ka)·(Ka/T) — forgetting to normalize
questions.solution_method: Linearize heater power about operating point; draw SFG with nodes: ΔEr → Ka → J-factor → thermal block 1/(RCs+1) → ΔT, with Kt feedback. Apply Mason's formula for ΔT/ΔEr. For sensitivity, use S = (∂F/∂Ka)·(Ka/F); open-loop S=1, closed-loop S = 1/(1+loop gain); at DC set s=0.

questions.stem: Characteristic equation of a system is s³ + 5s² + 4s + Ks + 20 = 0. Draw a neat sketch of the root locus of the system for 0 < K < ∞, showing all necessary steps.
common_errors: Rewriting CE: poles from s³+5s²+4s+20=0 (K=0), zero from s²+1=0 not s+4
Missing asymptote angles: (2k+1)·180°/n-m
Not checking departure angles from complex poles
questions.solution_method: Rewrite CE as 1 + K·s/(s³+5s²+4s+20) = 0. Factor denominator at K=0: s³+5s²+4s+20 = (s²+4)(s+5), giving open-loop poles at ±j2 and −5; open-loop zero at s=0. Apply standard RL rules: n=3, m=1, 2 asymptotes at ±90°, centroid=(−5+0−0)/2=−2.5. Find imaginary-axis crossings via RH.

questions.stem: Sketch the Nyquist plot for the system with open loop TF G(s) = 2.5K / [s(1+0.4s)(0.2s+1)], choosing the appropriate Nyquist contour. Determine the range of K (K>0) for which the closed loop system is stable. Also determine the Gain Margin for K=3.
common_errors: Not handling the pole at origin: indent Nyquist contour to the right, adding semicircle at s=0
Incorrect real-axis crossing: set Im[G(jω)]=0 to find ωpc, then compute Re[G(jωpc)]
Sign error: Z = N + P; P=0 for this plant so N must be 0 for stability
questions.solution_method: G(s) = 2.5K/[s(1+0.4s)(1+0.2s)]. Indent around origin. For Nyquist plot: compute G(jω) = 2.5K/[jω(1+j0.4ω)(1+j0.2ω)]; find ωpc where phase = −180° → set Im/Re = tan(180°); solve: 1 − 0.08ω² = 0 → ωpc = 1/√0.08 ≈ 3.54 rad/s. Evaluate |G(jωpc)| = 2.5K/(3.54·…); for stability |G(jωpc)| < 1 → K < Kcritical. For K=3, GM = 1/|G(jωpc)|.

notes: First Semester 2010-11 comprehensive, course code AAOC C321. Total marks 120. Q1 and Q2 from page 1, Q3 and Q4 from page 2. Q3(a) references a block diagram figure with feedback transfer function (Kh·s+1); Kh value not explicitly stated — root contour parameter. All questions closed-book.
